titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD55/34

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=293 status=live modified=2021-04-06 11:29:50.487119+00 | translation moa=292 status=live modified=2021-04-06 11:29:48.642851+00

div.p:
Omnibus sancte matris Ecclesie filiis presentibus et futuris Rogerus de Scalebroc Salutem . Sciatis me concessisse et dedisse
et hac mea Carta confirmasse deo et Ecclesie Sancte Marie de Melros et monachis ibidem deo seruientibus pro salute et anima
domini mei Dunecani filii Gilleberti filii Fergus . et pro animabus omnium antecessorum et successorum eius . et pro salute anime mee et omnium
parentum et heredum meorum in liberam et puram et perpetuam Elemosinam piscaturam meam in Ostio fluminis de Don super
harenam . et unam salinam . et unam patellam ubicumque uoluerint in tota terra mea de Grenan . et ligna de proximo bosco meo
predicte piscature et saline et usibus fratrum et hominum suorum ibidem manentium modis omnibus sufficientia . Concessi etiam eis unum
Toftum ad inhabitandum iuxta litus maris ubi uoluerint . et alium Toftum subtus Castellum meum de Grenan ubi possint res
suas et Catalla reponere . et illam particulam terre arabilis que est ad occidentalem partem ueteris ulmi uersus antiquam
salinam . et pasturam sex bobus . et decem vaccis sufficientem . Hec omnia concessi et dedi predictis monachi libera et quieta et
soluta ab omni terreno seruitio et consuetudine et exactionem seculari . et uolo ut ea habeant teneant et possideant in perpetuum
ita libere et quiete plenarie et honorifice sicut aliquam Elemosinam liberius et quietius . plenius et honorificentius tenent uel
possident . Et ipsi monachi utentur omnibus prescriptis ad libitum suum modis omnibus sicut melius et utilius sibi uisum fuerit et
fratribus et hominibus suis . et ego et heredes mei warantizabimus eis hec omnia contra omnes homines et adquietabimus de omni
seruitio quod pro aliqua Elemosina exigi possit . Testibus . domino Iocelino . Glasguesni . episcopo . Symone archidiacono . Dunecano . domino meo . Walter . Corbet .

To all sons of Holy Mother Church, present and future, Roger of Skelbrooke sends Greeting: Know me to have granted, and to have given, and by this my charter to have made firm, to God and to the church of St Mary at Melrose and to the monks serving God in that place, for the salvation and the soul of my lord Duncan son of Gilbert son of Fergus, and for the souls of all his ancestors and successors, and for the salvation of my own soul and of all my kindred and heirs, in free and pure and perpetual alms, my fishery in the mouth of the River Doon above Harenam, and one saltpan, and one pan, wheresoever they might wish in the whole of my land of Greenan, and sufficient timber from my wood near the said fishery and saltpan and for the enjoyment in all ways of the brethren and their own men abiding there. I have also granted to them one toft to inhabit where they will near the sea shore, and another toft below my castle at Greenan where they can keep their moveable goods and cattle, and the parcel of arable land that is to the west side of the old elm towards the former saltpan, and pasture sufficient for six oxen and ten cows. All these things have I granted and given to the said monks free and quit and released from all earthly service, custom and secular exaction. And I will that they have, hold and possess them for all time in this wise freely and quietly, fully and honourably, just as freely and quietly, fully and honourably as they hold or possess any alms. And the monks shall make use of all things described before as it pleases them, and by all means, just as it might seem good and useful to them and to their brethren and their men. And I and my heirs shall warrant to them all these things against all men and we shall acquit every service which could be exacted in return for any alms. As witnesses, the lord Jocelin, bishop of Glasgow, Simon the archdeacon, Duncan my lord, Walter Corbet.
